Details: Removal of microcystins in water by coagulation and clarification can be little to moderately effective (96 percent maximum reported). This maximum value was reported in a study looking at algal toxin cell viability in conventional treatment with the addition of ferrate (VI) as a coagulant and oxidant. Other studies indicate much lower effectiveness (maximum removal of ~42%) for conventional treatment processes. The process effectiveness is dependent on initial toxin concentrations and the form of the toxin (intracellular versus extracellular). Conventional treatment is most effective at removing cell bound (intracellular) microcystins. Dissolved (extracellular) microcystins are not well removed through this process. If conventional treatment conditions promote cell lysis, conventional treatment may actually increase dissolved microcystins
